---
title: "Skein Lite"
pagetitle: "Skein Lite — an agentic RAG platform"
toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.eyebrow}
Retrieval-augmented generation · built end to end
:::
# A shopping assistant that knows when it doesn't know {.hero-title}

::: {.lede}
Skein Lite is a local-first, domain-swappable RAG platform. The demo is a storefront — an apparel brand called Aster Athletics with an assistant named Aria — but the engine underneath knows nothing about apparel. Swap one folder and it becomes a different store.
:::
:::

::: {.stat-strip}
::: {.stat}
::: {.n}
354
:::
::: {.k}
tests, all green
:::
:::
::: {.stat}
::: {.n}
5
:::
::: {.k}
retrieval + graph + metric layers
:::
:::
::: {.stat}
::: {.n}
1.0
:::
::: {.k}
CI eval-gate score
:::
:::
::: {.stat}
::: {.n}
0
:::
::: {.k}
domain terms in engine code
:::
:::
:::

The interesting problem in a retrieval system is not answering — it is refusing. A model that always answers will confidently invent an order number, a price, or a color it never saw. Most of the work here is in the seams around the model: a confidence gate that abstains on thin evidence, a governed metric layer for anything numeric, a knowledge graph for relationships, and a set of deterministic guards for the things a prompt can't be trusted to enforce — customer privacy, prompt injection, gender-correct recommendations.

The assistant is grounded in synthetic data for a fictional brand. Every product, review, and order is invented. What's real is the machinery: the ingestion, the hybrid retrieval, the evaluation harness, and the CI that blocks a regression before it ships.

## What to read next

::: {.grid-cards}
::: {.card}
### [Architecture →](architecture.qmd)
The path a question takes from keypress to grounded answer: hybrid retrieval, the reranker, the graph and metric seams, and the domain-agnostic engine.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Evaluation →](evaluation.qmd)
Four layers of quality control — an offline CI gate, RAGAS on a golden set, drift monitors, and an eval-gated model-promotion flow — and what each one actually catches.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Decisions →](decisions.qmd)
Why Cohere over the alternatives, why Groq for the LLM, why a graph at all, and where I'd switch if the constraints changed.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Reliability →](reliability.qmd)
The failure modes I planned for: what happens when the LLM, the vector store, or the voice service is slow or down, and the fallback for each.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Try it →](usage.qmd)
How to run it locally with no API keys, and how to talk to the assistant — the things it does well and the honest edges.
:::
::: {.card}
### [Source →](https://github.com/hoomanesteki/agentic-rag-knowledge-ai-platform)
The full codebase: engine, domain packs, evaluation, the Next.js storefront, and the CI that keeps it honest.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**A note on honesty.** This site documents what the system does, including where it is weak. The [Reliability](reliability.qmd) page lists the failure modes and the residual issues an adversarial review found and I chose to accept. A portfolio that only lists wins is a portfolio that stopped looking.
:::
